An almost untouched world in the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]], '''J't'p'tan''' (known to most simply as '''Doornik-628E''') was settled by the [[Galandan]] (from [[Galand]]), a population of whom were followers of a religious movement known as [[H'kig]] and were fleeing a conflict on [[Rishi/Legends|Rishi]]. The planet was named after four sacred glyphs: "[[Jeh]]" (the Immanent), "[[Teh]]" (the Transcendent), "[[Peh]]" (the Eternal), and "[[Tan]]" (the Conscious Essence). Only the last of these glyphs was considered secular enough to be spelled-out fully.

[[File:JtptanLandscape-TEGtPaM.png|thumb|left|250px|J't'p'tan landscape, around the Temple of the Infinite Spirit]]
For years, the H'kig shaped and moved stone to create the [[Temple of the Infinite Spirit]], a great place of worship. In the waning days of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]], a [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-sensitive group, the [[Fallanassi/Legends|Fallanassi]], fled the world of [[Lucazec/Legends|Lucazec]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] and arrived on J't'p'tan. When the [[Yevetha]] began to carry out their "[[Black Fleet Crisis|Great Purge]]," the Fallanassi protected the H'kig and their temple by creating the [[Force Illusion|illusion]] that the temple had already been destroyed, and there was nothing left to kill. When the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] defeated the Yevetha, the Fallanassi left the world, and J't'p'tan once again fell into anonymity.
